description: IB II, 61. Letter from Ḥalfon and Bundār b. Maḍmūn, in Aden, to Sulaymān b. Abū Zikrī Kohen, in Fustat. Dating: ca. 1150 CE. In which Maḍmūn’s two sons express their sympathy on the death of Abū Zikrī Kohen (r1–27) and discuss his unfinished business. Sulaymān had sent the writers a power of attorney to collect a debt from a Jewish merchant who has absconded without repaying (r27–37). They express their strong disapproval of the three wraps and scarf that Sulaymān had sent them, which are of mediocre quality (r37–51). Their father had sent much better items (r52–rm1). Their own father is gravely ill. Maḥrūz's widow and children are fine, but Maḥrūz's daughter had died two years before (rm2–v5).
tags: Illness, ib-partial translation-eng, IB2, India, illness letter 969-1517
description_he: ספר הודו II,61: מכתב מחלפון ובונדאר בני מדמון, מעדן, לסולימאן בן אבו זכרי, על אבל ועסקים, כ-1150
